examples.title: Contiguous File Allocation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm for allocating files using contiguous allocation method
examples.code:
ALGORITHM AllocateFile(FileName, FileSize)
BEGIN
  RequiredBlocks ← FileSize / BlockSize
  StartBlock ← FindContiguousFreeBlocks(RequiredBlocks)
  
  IF StartBlock = -1 THEN
    PRINT "Insufficient contiguous space"
    RETURN FAILURE
  END IF
  
  FOR i FROM 0 TO RequiredBlocks-1 DO
    MarkBlockAllocated(StartBlock + i)
    UpdateFAT(StartBlock + i, StartBlock + i + 1)
  END FOR
  
  UpdateDirectory(FileName, StartBlock, FileSize)
  RETURN SUCCESS
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm finds a contiguous region of free blocks large enough for the file. It marks each block as allocated and updates the directory with starting block and file size.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for searching for contiguous blocks, O(1) for allocation

examples.title: Linked File Allocation - Read Block
examples.description: Algorithm for reading a specific block using linked allocation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ReadBlock(FileDescriptor, BlockNumber, Buffer)
BEGIN
  CurrentBlock ← FileDescriptor.StartBlock
  TargetBlock ← BlockNumber
  
  IF CurrentBlock = NULL THEN
    RETURN ERROR "File is empty"
  END IF
  
  FOR i FROM 0 TO TargetBlock-1 DO
    CurrentBlock ← ReadPointer(CurrentBlock)
    IF CurrentBlock = NULL THEN
      RETURN ERROR "Block does not exist"
    END IF
  END FOR
  
  ReadDiskBlock(CurrentBlock, Buffer)
  RETURN SUCCESS
END
examples.explanation: To read a specific block in linked allocation, we must traverse from the beginning, following pointers until we reach the target block. This demonstrates why linked allocation is slow for random access.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the block number to read

examples.title: Indexed File Allocation - Read Block
examples.description: Algorithm for reading a block using indexed allocation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ReadBlockIndexed(FileDescriptor, BlockNumber, Buffer)
BEGIN
  IndexBlock ← FileDescriptor.IndexBlockPointer
  
  ReadDiskBlock(IndexBlock, IndexTable)
  
  IF BlockNumber >= MaxBlocks THEN
    RETURN ERROR "Block number exceeds file limit"
  END IF
  
  DataBlock ← IndexTable[BlockNumber]
  
  IF DataBlock = 0 THEN
    RETURN ERROR "Block not allocated"
  END IF
  
  ReadDiskBlock(DataBlock, Buffer)
  RETURN SUCCESS
END
examples.explanation: Indexed allocation provides O(1) access to any block by reading the index block first, then directly accessing the target data block using the pointer stored in the index.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) - constant time access to any block

examples.title: Bit Vector Free Space Management
examples.description: Implementation of free space management using bit vector (bitmap)
examples.explanation: This implementation shows how bit vectors work - scanning for 0 (free) to allocate and setting to 1 (allocated). It provides O(1) allocation check and efficient memory usage.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for allocate_block in worst case, O(1) for free_block
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of blocks
